competences.name: Business Situation Analysis
competences.description: Analysing business situations to identify problems, opportunities and feasibility of solutions.

levels.description: Assists in gathering information about business situations under supervision.
Collects and documents information about current business situations with guidance.
Investigates and documents business situations, identifies options and contributes to recommendations.
Analyses business situations to identify problems and opportunities; formulates and evaluates options and produces recommendations.
Leads complex business situation analysis across multiple domains; develops and drives adoption of analysis approaches and best practices.

competences.name: Organisational Facilitation
competences.description: Planning and leading group activities to achieve productive outcomes and consensus.

levels.description: Participates in facilitated sessions and supports logistics under direction.
Facilitates small team discussions with a clear agenda and defined outcomes.
Facilitates workshops and meetings across teams, manages group dynamics and drives outcomes.
Plans and leads complex facilitation sessions across organisational boundaries with senior stakeholders.
Leads organisational facilitation strategy; mentors facilitators and embeds facilitation as an organisational capability.

competences.name: Stakeholder Relationship Management
competences.description: Influencing attitudes and decisions of business and technology stakeholders.

levels.description: Communicates with immediate stakeholders in a clear and professional manner.
Identifies stakeholders and supports relationship management activities under guidance.
Builds and maintains relationships with key stakeholders; manages expectations and resolves routine conflicts.
Manages complex stakeholder relationships across organisational boundaries; negotiates and influences decisions.
Develops and leads stakeholder engagement strategy at senior and executive levels across the organisation.

competences.name: Innovation
competences.description: Applying creative thinking to generate new ideas, methods and solutions.

levels.description: Contributes ideas and suggestions in team discussions.
Proposes new approaches to solve defined problems within own area.
Generates and evaluates innovative solutions; pilots new ideas and captures learnings.
Leads innovation initiatives; creates an environment that encourages creativity and experimentation.
Sets innovation strategy; builds organisational innovation capability and drives transformative change.

competences.name: Organisational Capability Development
competences.description: Providing leadership, guidance and management to enable the organisation to develop its capabilities.

levels.description: Participates in capability development activities such as training and knowledge sharing.
Contributes to team capability by sharing knowledge and supporting colleagues.
Identifies capability gaps and recommends targeted development activities.
Designs and implements capability development programmes for teams or functions.
Leads organisational capability development strategy; builds long-term talent pipelines and institutional knowledge.

competences.name: Enterprise & Business Architecture
competences.description: Creating, iterating and maintaining the enterprise architecture and ensuring alignment with business strategy.

levels.description: Understands basic architecture concepts and assists in documentation tasks.
Produces architecture documentation and models under guidance.
Contributes to architecture designs, identifies gaps and documents current and target states.
Designs and maintains enterprise architecture views; ensures alignment with strategy and standards.
Leads enterprise architecture practice; owns the architectural vision and governs strategic alignment across the organisation.

competences.name: Solution Architecture
competences.description: Providing guidance on the design and implementation of technology solutions.

levels.description: Assists in documenting solution designs and existing systems under supervision.
Produces low-level solution documentation and contributes to design reviews.
Designs solutions for moderately complex systems; validates against non-functional requirements.
Leads solution design across complex programmes; ensures architectural integrity and technical coherence.
Defines solution architecture standards and patterns; governs technical decisions across multiple programmes.

competences.name: Systems Design
competences.description: Designing systems and components to meet specified requirements.

levels.description: Understands basic design principles and assists in producing design artefacts.
Produces detailed designs for simple components with guidance.
Designs systems components to meet functional and non-functional requirements.
Leads design activities for complex systems; ensures scalability, security and maintainability.
Defines system design standards and approaches; oversees design quality across large-scale programmes.

competences.name: Data Modelling & Design
competences.description: Defining, designing and maintaining data models and data structures.

levels.description: Understands basic data modelling concepts and assists in creating simple models.
Creates data models for simple domains under guidance; documents data entities and relationships.
Develops and validates data models for moderately complex domains; applies normalisation principles.
Leads data modelling activities; designs enterprise-wide data structures aligned to business needs.
Defines data architecture standards; governs data models across the organisation and leads master data strategy.

competences.name: Strategic Planning
competences.description: Creating and maintaining technology strategy and roadmaps aligned to business goals.

levels.description: Gathers and organises information to support strategic planning activities.
Contributes analysis and insight to support technology strategy development.
Develops technology roadmaps for specific domains; aligns plans to business priorities.
Leads technology strategy development; produces multi-year plans and gains senior buy-in.
Owns organisational technology strategy; drives alignment across business units and sets long-term direction.

competences.name: Programming & Software Development
competences.description: Developing, testing and maintaining software applications using appropriate languages and frameworks.

levels.description: Writes simple code with guidance, following established patterns and standards.
Develops straightforward software components with limited supervision; fixes basic defects.
Develops complex features independently; conducts code reviews and applies best practices.
Leads development of major system components; defines coding standards and mentors junior developers.
Drives engineering excellence across teams; evaluates and adopts emerging languages and frameworks.

competences.name: Software Design
competences.description: Designing software structures, components and interfaces to meet requirements.

levels.description: Assists in documenting software designs using established templates.
Produces detailed low-level designs for simple components under guidance.
Designs and documents software modules; applies design patterns and evaluates trade-offs.
Leads software design for complex systems; ensures consistency, reusability and maintainability.
Defines software design principles and standards; influences architectural decisions across the organisation.

competences.name: Testing
competences.description: Designing, executing and reporting tests to ensure software quality and reliability.

levels.description: Executes predefined test cases and records results under supervision.
Designs and executes test cases for simple features; identifies and logs defects.
Plans and executes testing for complex features; analyses defects and recommends improvements.
Defines testing strategy for programmes; introduces automation and ensures test coverage.
Leads quality engineering practice; sets testing standards and drives shift-left quality culture.

competences.name: Configuration Management
competences.description: Planning, implementing and managing configuration and version control of software and infrastructure.

levels.description: Uses version control tools under supervision to commit and retrieve code.
Manages branches and merges in version control; follows configuration management procedures.
Implements configuration management plans; automates build and release processes.
Defines configuration management strategy; manages tooling and processes across programmes.
Governs configuration management across the organisation; drives automation and compliance.

competences.name: Systems Integration & Build
competences.description: Planning, implementing and controlling activities to integrate system components.

levels.description: Assists in integration tasks under supervision, following defined procedures.
Integrates simple components; troubleshoots basic integration issues.
Plans and executes integration of moderately complex systems; identifies and resolves integration defects.
Leads integration planning for complex systems; defines integration standards and test approaches.
Governs integration architecture and practices; ensures seamless interoperability across enterprise systems.

competences.name: Data Engineering
competences.description: Designing, building and maintaining data pipelines, stores and infrastructure.

levels.description: Assists with data pipeline tasks under supervision using established tools.
Builds and maintains simple data pipelines; troubleshoots data quality issues.
Designs and implements robust data pipelines; optimises for performance and reliability.
Leads data engineering for complex platforms; defines standards for data ingestion and transformation.
Architects enterprise data engineering capabilities; drives data platform strategy and adoption.

competences.name: Data Science
competences.description: Applying statistical and machine learning methods to extract insights from data.

levels.description: Assists with data preparation and exploratory analysis under supervision.
Applies standard statistical and ML techniques to defined business problems.
Develops, validates and deploys models; communicates findings to non-technical stakeholders.
Leads data science initiatives; designs experimentation frameworks and ensures model governance.
Sets data science strategy; establishes centres of excellence and drives organisation-wide data literacy.

competences.name: Data Management
competences.description: Developing and implementing plans, policies and practices that control, protect and optimise data assets.

levels.description: Follows data management procedures and assists in maintaining data registers.
Maintains data catalogues and supports data quality monitoring activities.
Implements data management policies; monitors data quality and resolves data issues.
Designs and leads data governance frameworks; ensures regulatory compliance and data stewardship.
Defines enterprise data management strategy; owns data governance policies and drives data maturity.

competences.name: Machine Learning
competences.description: Designing, developing and deploying machine learning models and systems.

levels.description: Understands basic ML concepts; assists in data preparation and model evaluation tasks.
Trains and evaluates standard ML models using established frameworks.
Designs and deploys ML models; tunes hyperparameters and monitors model performance in production.
Leads ML projects end-to-end; implements MLOps practices and ensures model reliability at scale.
Defines ML strategy and architecture; establishes model governance and drives responsible AI practices.

competences.name: Data Visualisation
competences.description: Designing and producing visual representations of data to communicate insights.

levels.description: Creates basic charts and reports using standard tools under supervision.
Develops dashboards and visualisations for routine reporting needs.
Designs interactive visualisations tailored to audience needs; ensures accuracy and clarity.
Leads data visualisation strategy; defines standards and ensures visualisations drive actionable decisions.
Sets enterprise data storytelling standards; embeds visual analytics culture across the organisation.

competences.name: Release & Deployment
competences.description: Managing the process of packaging, building and deploying releases to environments.

levels.description: Supports release activities under supervision; follows deployment checklists.
Executes release and deployment procedures; identifies and escalates deployment issues.
Plans and coordinates releases; implements CI/CD pipelines and rollback procedures.
Defines release management strategy; drives automation and zero-downtime deployment practices.
Governs release engineering practice; sets standards for continuous delivery at enterprise scale.

competences.name: Systems & Software Life Cycle Engineering
competences.description: Establishing and deploying processes that support effective development and maintenance of systems.

levels.description: Follows defined life cycle processes and assists in process documentation.
Applies life cycle processes to assigned tasks; flags process gaps to senior team members.
Implements life cycle processes for projects; contributes to process improvement initiatives.
Defines and improves life cycle processes across programmes; aligns practices with standards.
Owns life cycle engineering strategy; drives organisational adoption of engineering excellence practices.

competences.name: IT Infrastructure
competences.description: Deploying, configuring, maintaining and supporting IT infrastructure components.

levels.description: Performs routine infrastructure tasks under close supervision.
Maintains and troubleshoots infrastructure components; follows established runbooks.
Configures and optimises infrastructure; implements monitoring and alerting.
Designs and manages infrastructure platforms; introduces infrastructure-as-code practices.
Defines infrastructure architecture strategy; drives cloud adoption and automation at scale.

competences.name: Information Security
competences.description: Selecting, designing, justifying and implementing security controls to manage risks.

levels.description: Follows security procedures and reports security incidents under supervision.
Applies security controls in daily work; identifies and escalates security risks.
Implements security controls; conducts risk assessments and supports audits.
Leads security design for systems and programmes; defines security architecture and standards.
Owns information security strategy; drives security culture and governs enterprise risk posture.

competences.name: Security Operations
competences.description: Monitoring, detecting and responding to security events and incidents.

levels.description: Monitors security alerts using established tools; escalates incidents following defined procedures.
Triages and responds to routine security incidents; maintains security monitoring systems.
Investigates complex security incidents; performs threat hunting and improves detection rules.
Leads security operations centre activities; defines detection and response playbooks.
Defines security operations strategy; integrates threat intelligence and drives security automation.

competences.name: Risk Management
competences.description: Planning and implementing organisation-wide processes and procedures for the management of risk.

levels.description: Assists in risk identification and documentation activities under supervision.
Identifies and documents risks for assigned areas; supports risk treatment activities.
Conducts risk assessments; maintains risk registers and monitors mitigation actions.
Leads risk management programmes; defines risk appetite and governance frameworks.
Owns enterprise risk management strategy; integrates risk into strategic decision-making.

competences.name: Information Assurance
competences.description: Protecting against and managing risks related to the use, storage, and transmission of data and information systems.

levels.description: Follows information assurance policies and assists in compliance checks.
Implements information assurance controls; supports compliance assessments and audits.
Assesses information assurance risks; develops and implements remediation plans.
Leads information assurance programmes; ensures regulatory and policy compliance across systems.
Defines information assurance strategy; owns compliance frameworks and drives a security-first culture.

competences.name: Project Management
competences.description: Delivering defined project outputs on time, to budget and to agreed quality standards.

levels.description: Supports project activities and maintains project documentation under supervision.
Manages small tasks or workstreams; tracks progress and escalates issues.
Manages projects of moderate complexity; plans, monitors and controls scope, time and cost.
Leads complex projects; manages risks, stakeholders and cross-functional dependencies.
Sponsors or governs major programmes; defines delivery standards and builds project management capability.

competences.name: Programme Management
competences.description: Identifying, planning and coordinating a set of related projects towards a strategic objective.

levels.description: Supports programme administration and reporting activities.
Coordinates activities across small workstreams; maintains programme documentation.
Manages interdependencies across projects; consolidates programme reporting and manages risks.
Leads strategic programmes; manages benefits, governance and senior stakeholder engagement.
Defines programme management standards; governs transformational change programmes at enterprise scale.

competences.name: Benefits Management
competences.description: Identifying, planning, managing and realising benefits from projects and programmes.

levels.description: Assists in documenting expected benefits for projects under supervision.
Maintains benefits registers; supports baseline measurement activities.
Tracks and reports benefit realisation; identifies risks to benefit delivery.
Leads benefits management for programmes; designs measurement frameworks and drives realisation.
Defines enterprise benefits management strategy; embeds a culture of outcome-focused delivery.

competences.name: Resource Management
competences.description: Planning, deploying and monitoring the use of human resources to meet organisational needs.

levels.description: Maintains resource trackers and supports scheduling activities under supervision.
Coordinates resource allocation for small teams; flags resource conflicts.
Plans and manages resource demand and supply for projects; negotiates resource availability.
Leads resource management across programmes; optimises utilisation and builds capacity plans.
Defines workforce strategy; integrates resource management with financial planning and talent development.

competences.name: Performance Management
competences.description: Driving high performance through goal-setting, feedback, coaching and development conversations.

levels.description: Receives and applies feedback constructively; sets personal development goals.
Supports team members with feedback and monitors individual progress.
Sets clear performance goals for direct reports; conducts regular reviews and addresses underperformance.
Leads performance management culture across a function; aligns individual goals to organisational strategy.
Defines performance management frameworks; drives a high-performance culture across the organisation.

competences.name: Professional Development
competences.description: Facilitating the professional growth of individuals through learning and career guidance.

levels.description: Identifies own learning needs and participates in development activities.
Supports colleagues in identifying learning opportunities; shares knowledge proactively.
Creates development plans for team members; connects individuals with learning resources and opportunities.
Leads professional development programmes for a function; builds career pathways and mentoring networks.
Defines talent and professional development strategy; builds learning culture and succession pipelines.

competences.name: Learning & Development Management
competences.description: Managing and delivering activities to develop skills, knowledge and competencies across the organisation.

levels.description: Assists in coordinating learning events and maintaining training records.
Delivers training sessions and supports learning programme administration.
Designs and delivers learning programmes; evaluates effectiveness and iterates content.
Leads L&D strategy for a function; manages learning platforms and measures ROI.
Defines enterprise-wide L&D strategy; builds learning ecosystems and embeds continuous development culture.

competences.name: Competency Assessment
competences.description: Assessing and evaluating the competencies of individuals against defined frameworks.

levels.description: Participates in competency assessments and provides self-assessment input.
Assists in conducting competency assessments using defined tools and criteria.
Conducts competency assessments independently; produces development recommendations.
Leads competency assessment programmes; calibrates assessors and ensures consistency.
Defines competency frameworks and assessment methodology; governs capability benchmarking across the organisation.

competences.name: Workforce Planning
competences.description: Estimating the demand for people and skills and planning to meet that demand.

levels.description: Gathers data to support workforce planning analysis under supervision.
Maintains workforce data and produces routine headcount and skills reports.
Analyses workforce supply and demand; develops short-term workforce plans.
Leads strategic workforce planning; integrates with financial and business planning cycles.
Defines enterprise workforce strategy; models long-term capability needs and drives talent acquisition strategy.

competences.name: Incident Management
competences.description: Coordinating responses to service incidents to restore normal operation as quickly as possible.

levels.description: Logs incidents and follows defined procedures to escalate issues.
Investigates and resolves routine incidents; communicates status to affected users.
Manages complex incidents; coordinates resolution across teams and produces incident reports.
Leads incident management capability; defines processes, tooling and SLAs for incident response.
Owns major incident management strategy; drives continual service improvement and resilience planning.

competences.name: Problem Management
competences.description: Minimising the impact of incidents by identifying root causes and implementing permanent fixes.

levels.description: Assists in documenting problem records and known errors.
Investigates routine problems; contributes to root cause analysis activities.
Leads root cause analysis for complex problems; drives permanent fixes and proactive problem prevention.
Defines problem management processes; uses trend analysis to eliminate recurring incidents.
Sets problem management strategy; embeds proactive problem prevention culture across IT operations.

competences.name: Service Level Management
competences.description: Negotiating, agreeing and monitoring service level agreements and ensuring services meet targets.

levels.description: Collects and records service performance data under supervision.
Monitors service levels against agreed targets; produces performance reports.
Negotiates and manages SLAs with internal teams; identifies and addresses performance gaps.
Defines SLA frameworks; manages vendor relationships and drives continuous service improvement.
Sets service management strategy; governs SLA governance across all services and suppliers.

competences.name: Supplier Management
competences.description: Aligning supplier performance to the needs of the business and maintaining productive supplier relationships.

levels.description: Assists in maintaining supplier records and tracking purchase orders.
Monitors supplier deliverables and escalates performance issues.
Manages supplier relationships; conducts performance reviews and manages contract compliance.
Leads strategic supplier management; negotiates contracts and drives supplier development.
Defines supplier management strategy; manages key strategic partnerships and optimises supply chain.

competences.name: Requirements Definition & Management
competences.description: Defining, managing and communicating business and technical requirements throughout the project lifecycle.

levels.description: Assists in documenting requirements using templates under supervision.
Elicits and documents straightforward requirements; supports requirements review sessions.
Leads requirements workshops; manages requirements through the project lifecycle and handles changes.
Defines requirements management standards; resolves conflicting requirements across complex programmes.
Governs requirements practices across the organisation; drives adoption of model-based approaches.

competences.name: Business Process Improvement
competences.description: Evaluating and redesigning business processes to improve efficiency, quality and outcomes.

levels.description: Documents existing processes using defined templates under supervision.
Analyses and maps current state processes; identifies waste and inefficiencies.
Designs improved processes; facilitates workshops and validates improvements with stakeholders.
Leads process transformation initiatives; applies Lean or Six Sigma methods and manages change.
Defines business process management strategy; drives enterprise-wide process excellence culture.

competences.name: Feasibility Assessment
competences.description: Evaluating the viability and risks of proposed solutions and investment options.

levels.description: Gathers and organises data to support feasibility studies under supervision.
Contributes to feasibility assessments by producing analysis on defined dimensions.
Conducts feasibility assessments for options; produces structured business cases with recommendations.
Leads complex feasibility studies; applies investment appraisal techniques and presents to senior stakeholders.
Defines feasibility assessment standards; governs business case quality across the organisation.

competences.name: Organisational Change Management
competences.description: Planning and leading people-focused change to achieve and sustain business outcomes.

levels.description: Participates in change activities and supports communication tasks under supervision.
Delivers change communications and training; supports impact assessments.
Develops change management plans; conducts stakeholder analysis and manages resistance.
Leads change management for complex transformations; integrates change with programme delivery.
Defines organisational change capability; builds change maturity and champions transformation culture.

competences.name: Knowledge Management
competences.description: Systematically managing the creation, sharing, retention and application of knowledge.

levels.description: Follows knowledge management procedures; contributes to wikis and knowledge bases.
Creates and maintains knowledge articles; ensures content accuracy and relevance.
Designs and implements knowledge management processes; promotes knowledge sharing across teams.
Leads knowledge management strategy for a function; implements tools and measures knowledge effectiveness.
Defines enterprise knowledge management strategy; builds learning organisation capabilities.

competences.name: Consultancy
competences.description: Providing advice and recommendations to address client needs with authority and credibility.

levels.description: Supports consultancy engagements by gathering information and preparing materials.
Contributes analysis and recommendations to defined consultancy assignments.
Manages consultancy assignments independently; builds client relationships and delivers actionable recommendations.
Leads complex consultancy engagements; develops trusted advisor relationships at senior levels.
Defines consultancy practice standards; grows the consultancy capability and drives business development.

competences.name: User Experience Analysis
competences.description: Analysing user needs and contexts to inform design decisions.

levels.description: Assists in user research activities such as note-taking and participant recruitment.
Conducts basic user interviews and usability tests; synthesises findings.
Plans and leads user research; produces personas, journey maps and actionable insights.
Defines user research strategy; builds research programmes and integrates insights into design processes.
Leads UX research practice; drives evidence-based design culture across the organisation.

competences.name: User Experience Design
competences.description: Designing products and services that provide meaningful, accessible and satisfying experiences.

levels.description: Produces wireframes and prototypes under supervision using established tools.
Designs user interfaces for simple features; applies design system components.
Designs end-to-end user experiences for complex products; iterates based on user research and testing.
Leads UX design for products or platforms; defines design principles and mentors junior designers.
Defines UX design strategy; builds and governs design systems and sets experience standards.

competences.name: Product Management
competences.description: Managing the development of products and services to meet user needs and business goals.

levels.description: Supports product team with backlog management and sprint planning activities.
Maintains product backlog; writes user stories and acceptance criteria with guidance.
Owns product backlog; prioritises features based on user feedback and business value.
Leads product strategy; manages product roadmap and aligns stakeholders around vision.
Defines portfolio-level product strategy; drives product-led growth and builds product management capability.

competences.name: Employee Experience
competences.description: Creating and promoting a positive, engaging and productive work environment.

levels.description: Contributes to a positive team environment and supports colleagues.
Actively promotes inclusive behaviours; surfaces team concerns to managers.
Implements initiatives to improve team wellbeing and engagement; monitors sentiment.
Designs employee experience programmes; leads culture and engagement improvements.
Defines employee experience strategy; embeds values-driven culture across the organisation.

competences.name: Measurement
competences.description: Developing and implementing consistent approaches to measurement and metrics.

levels.description: Collects and records data for defined metrics under supervision.
Maintains dashboards and produces routine metrics reports.
Designs measurement frameworks for teams; selects KPIs aligned to objectives.
Leads measurement strategy for a programme or function; drives data-driven decision making.
Defines enterprise measurement and analytics strategy; embeds evidence-based management practices.

competences.name: Methods & Tools
competences.description: Ensuring that effective methods and tools are adopted and applied across projects and teams.

levels.description: Uses defined methods and tools as directed; follows established procedures.
Applies methods and tools proficiently; provides guidance to peers on standard usage.
Evaluates and recommends methods and tools; supports adoption and training.
Defines methods and tooling standards for a function; governs adoption and measures effectiveness.
Sets enterprise methods and tooling strategy; drives continuous improvement in engineering and delivery practices.
